message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #1 pushed, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #1 (password toggle fix) committed and pushed (fe1009a). CI is running.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review the changes and send your consent ("I consent") or specific concerns. We need 9/9 consensus before Lea moves to Task #2.

Review the commit: static/js/password-toggle.js and tests/api/register.rs were modified. The fix adds a hidden input that mirrors the password value when the confirm field is removed by the toggle.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the changes from commit fe1009a.

input.description: Show files changed in commit fe1009a

content.content:
commit fe1009abb1bb596f32bbfcd46fa39e614e33a3e8
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:20:38 2026 -0800

    Fix password toggle: add hidden input mirror for password_confirmation
    
    When the password toggle removes the confirm field from the DOM, the form
    no longer sends password_confirmation, causing the server to reject the
    submission. Add a hidden input that mirrors the password value whenever
    the confirm group is removed, and sync it on input events.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/js/password-toggle.js | 29 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
 tests/api/register.rs        |  4 ++++
 2 files changed, 30 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

content.content:
commit fe1009abb1bb596f32bbfcd46fa39e614e33a3e8
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:20:38 2026 -0800

    Fix password toggle: add hidden input mirror for password_confirmation
    
    When the password toggle removes the confirm field from the DOM, the form
    no longer sends password_confirmation, causing the server to reject the
    submission. Add a hidden input that mirrors the password value whenever
    the confirm group is removed, and sync it on input events.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/tests/api/register.rs b/tests/api/register.rs
index 0018724..81425f4 100644
--- a/tests/api/register.rs
+++ b/tests/api/register.rs
@@ -334,6 +334,10 @@ async fn password_toggle_script_is_served() {
         body.contains("aria-label"),
         "Script should set aria-label for accessibility"
     );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("password_confirmation"),
+        "Script should create a hidden input for password_confirmation"
+    );
 }
 
 #[tokio::test]

content.text: Now let me read the full updated file to verify the complete picture.
Good. I've reviewed the full change. Now let me send my consent.

input.content:
**I consent** to Task #1 (commit fe1009a).

From a UI design perspective, this is clean. The hidden input is invisible to the user -- no visual impact at all. The behavior is correct: when the confirm field is removed from the DOM, the hidden input mirrors the password so the server gets what it expects.

A few things I verified:
- **No visual regression**: The hidden input has no visual footprint. The toggle button still sits cleanly under the password group.
- **State transitions are clean**: Toggle on (show password) removes confirm group, adds hidden mirror. Toggle off (hide password) restores confirm group, removes hidden mirror. The initial state on page load also correctly removes confirm and adds the mirror.
- **Input sync is handled**: The `input` event listener keeps the hidden value current as the user types. This covers the case where the user types *after* toggling.

No design concerns. Solid fix for a real functional gap.
input.summary: Consent on Task #1 - no visual concerns

routing.summary: Consent on Task #1 - no visual concerns
routing.content:
**I consent** to Task #1 (commit fe1009a).

From a UI design perspective, this is clean. The hidden input is invisible to the user -- no visual impact at all. The behavior is correct: when the confirm field is removed from the DOM, the hidden input mirrors the password so the server gets what it expects.

A few things I verified:
- **No visual regression**: The hidden input has no visual footprint. The toggle button still sits cleanly under the password group.
- **State transitions are clean**: Toggle on (show password) removes confirm group, adds hidden mirror. Toggle off (hide password) restores confirm group, removes hidden mirror. The initial state on page load also correctly removes confirm and adds the mirror.
- **Input sync is handled**: The `input` event listener keeps the hidden value current as the user types. This covers the case where the user types *after* toggling.

No design concerns. Solid fix for a real functional gap.

content.text: I'm standing by, waiting for the consensus results on Task #1 and for Lea to begin work on Task #2 (the CSS gaps I flagged). No action needed from me right now -- I've submitted my consent and am ready to review the next change when it comes.
